floor_layout: Single floor. Entry on the front wall, billing on the way out near the front. Power wall splits left (women's flow) and right (men's flow). Kids/infants at the back, accessible from both sides.

zones.name: Power wall & entry
zones.notes: Refresh cadence: weekly (Monday) in baseline. Hero SKUs sit here. Influences arrival-intent strength.

zones.name: Women's ethnic
zones.notes: Largest revenue zone. L/XL kurti and saree blouses are the first sizes to stock out at peak.

zones.name: Women's footwear & accessories
zones.notes: High margin (52-64%). In baseline it's treated as a destination — shoppers rarely cross over from ethnic. Lever 5 (cross-merch) targets this.

zones.name: Men's formal & ethnic
zones.notes: Diwali kurta sets concentrated here. Sherwani SKUs cross-sell with mojaris in mens_fa.

zones.name: Kids (boys + girls combined)
zones.notes: Kids ethnic spikes during Diwali. School-uniform-and-Diwali families arrive 11am-1pm.

zones.name: Infants & newborn
zones.notes: Smallest zone. Staffed by 1 person in baseline — this is the staff member that should be reallocated under an optimized policy.

zones.name: Billing & exit
zones.notes: 6 counters. Baseline: 2 open 10am-6pm, 3 open 6pm-10pm. Single straight queue feeds all 6.

kids_bank.notes: No dedicated kids cubicles in baseline.

baseline_cross_merch_friction.kurti_to_leggings: medium — different sub-zones within women's bank
baseline_cross_merch_friction.kurta_to_mojari: medium — adjacent but on separate racks
baseline_cross_merch_friction.ethnic_to_dupatta: high — dupatta sits in accessories, not at the ethnic rack

ac_state.notes: Reduced AC late evening to save cost — affects dwell and family-group exit speed.
